definition: Grimoire's delete and truncation mutators collapse specific token regions (SYSTEM-id, URI authority, attribute value, name token) to empty strings or NUL bytes within an otherwise valid document skeleton, producing degenerate zero-length or NULL fields that trigger otherwise-unreached code paths; cmplog is pinned to non-empty fixed literals and cannot manufacture this structural emptiness.

authoring_contract.goal: ONE parameterized template.c isolating this cluster's shared mechanism, with exactly ONE compile-time -D knob = the program-feature axis. params.json:parameter MUST name that macro; scan_values sweep it; fuzzers = involved_fuzzers; expected_direction = '<winner> > <loser>'.
authoring_contract.isolate: No partial-match coverage edges unless the mechanism IS a gradient (VP families). Keep the gate the only objective.

signature.mechanism_summary: Grimoire's GeneralizationStage abstracts byte runs into token/gap structures and structural mutators splice/recurse them, assembling the deep well-formed XML nesting with an empty-name attribute that flat havoc cannot build or preserve.
signature.one_line: Grimoire structural recombination builds empty-name XML to trigger namelen<=0 gate

analysis.summary_one_line: Well-formed XML with a zero-length name token (e.g. empty attribute n="") that drives xmlDictComputeBigKey with namelen<=0, taking the true return(0) branch.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A structurally valid XML fragment whose dictionary-interned tokens include a zero-length (or NULL) name, so that xmlDictComputeBigKey is invoked with namelen<=0 and takes the true branch (return(0)). The winner needs deep, repeated, well-formed element/attribute structure that survives parsing far enough to repeatedly intern names through the dict, including a degenerate empty name.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Grimoire winner seeds are clean, recursively-structured XML: '<l n=""><b k:f="><b r:f=&' with an explicit empty attribute value n="" and many repeated '<b k:f=' element/attribute frames. This degenerate empty-name token produces namelen<=0 at the dict-hash call. The branch's true side fires only under grimoire (T=22 vs T=0 for the loser), and grimoire invokes xmlDictComputeBigKey far more (W 334 entry-line hits vs L 105).
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Cmplog loser seeds are byte-corrupted, low-structure inputs ('IxlZ...n="1..l v' and runs of 0d/c5/ff filler) that fail to parse into the deep repeated element/attribute nesting needed to intern an empty name; they never take the true side (T=0 F=105) and call the hash function far less, so the namelen<=0 condition is never reached.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: Both cmplog and grimoire carry the same comparison-solving (input-to-state) stage, so the divergence is NOT comparison-solving. The single-technique delta is grimoire structural: its GeneralizationStage plus the Grimoire structural mutators, which generalize concrete byte runs into <GAP> token structures and splice/recurse on them, building and preserving the deep well-formed XML element/attribute nesting (including the empty-name token) that the flat havoc corpus of cmplog cannot assemble or keep coherent.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocking branch is the true side of a namelen<=0 || data==NULL guard inside the dict big-key hash function.
The globally blocked side is the true branch.
The winner takes the true branch (T=22) while the loser never does (T=0).
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L 363  T=22 F=312  T=0 F=105  if (namelen <= 0 || data == NULL) return(0);

evidence_trail.claim: The winner invokes xmlDictComputeBigKey far more often than the loser.
Grimoire winner seeds are clean repeated XML with an explicit empty attribute value n="".
Winner seeds repeat well-formed element/attribute frames.
Loser cmplog seeds are byte-corrupted, low-structure inputs that lack the deep repeated XML nesting.
The single-technique delta is grimoire adding generalization plus Grimoire mutators on top of cmplog, which already has I2S.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
The single-technique delta is therefore
`grimoire` vs `cmplog` (both have I2S; grimoire adds generalization +
Grimoire mutators), not vs naive.

evidence_trail.claim: Grimoire's generalization and structural mutators splice and recurse on generalized token/gap structures.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
splice and recurse on these generalised token/gap structures
(string-based, grammar-free structural mutation)

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness where the empty/zero-length name token is reachable by flat havoc+I2S byte mutation (no deep nesting required) and cmplog reaches the namelen<=0 branch at parity with grimoire, showing structural recombination is not what gates the true side.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: The mapping from the seed bytes n="" to a literal namelen<=0 at xmlDictComputeBigKey is inferred from XML semantics and hit-count divergence, not directly traced through the parser-to-dict call path.

signature.mechanism_summary: Grimoire's GeneralizationStage and structural mutators synthesize the multi-token xmlns-prefixed attribute with an empty value, producing the parsed empty val (val[0]==0) that cmplog's per-CMP byte substitution cannot construct structurally.
signature.one_line: Grimoire assembles xmlns: attribute with empty value to reach val[0]==0 check

analysis.summary_one_line: An xmlns-prefixed attribute (ns='xmlns') whose decoded value is empty, so val[0]==0 takes the true branch.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A well-formed XML element bearing a namespace-declaration attribute with the 'xmlns' prefix (e.g. xmlns:f="") whose value decodes to the empty string, so xmlSplitQName yields ns='xmlns' (matching the ns[0..5]=='xmlns'\0 gate at 1234-1236) and the decoded val is empty (val[0]==0 at 1261).
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Grimoire's generalization + structural recombination assembles valid XML containing the literal namespace construct: winner seed 2 contains '<b xmlns:f="">', an xmlns-prefixed attribute with an empty quoted value, which drives ns='xmlns' and an empty val into the blocker.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: The cmplog loser is stuck on a degenerate self-repeating byte blob '//+'/*:'*ff(' with no element/attribute grammar, so it never produces a parsed xmlns:-prefixed attribute with an empty value and stays on the false side.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: grimoire's GeneralizationStage abstracts corpus entries into token/gap structures and the Grimoire mutators splice/recurse on them, synthesizing the multi-token XML namespace-declaration construct (an xmlns:-prefixed attribute with empty value) that cmplog's per-CMP byte substitution cannot build; this is grimoire structural mutation.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the true side of val[0]==0 inside the xmlns-prefix namespace branch.
Reaching the blocker requires the parsed ns to equal the literal 'xmlns'.
A grimoire winner-resolving seed contains an xmlns-prefixed attribute with an empty value.
That same winner seed shows the empty quoted value f="" immediately after the xmlns: prefix.
The cmplog loser seed is a degenerate self-repeating blob with no XML namespace structure.
Grimoire generalizes corpus entries and splices token/gap structures, enabling grammar-free structural construction.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
splice and recurse on these generalised token/gap structures
(string-based, grammar-free structural mutation)

evidence_trail.claim: The decisive single-technique delta is grimoire (generalization + Grimoire mutators) over cmplog, both of which run I2S.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
grimoire` vs `cmplog` (both have I2S; grimoire adds generalization +
Grimoire mutators)

evidence_trail.claim: Grimoire resolves the branch in all 10 trials while cmplog blocks in 8.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
winner: resolved=10/10  blocked=0  unreached=0
  loser:  resolved=2/10  blocked=8  unreached=0

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness gating on an empty xmlns-prefixed attribute value where cmplog resolves the branch as often as grimoire (no grimoire>cmplog separation), showing structural recombination is not what builds the xmlns: construct.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: I infer the grimoire winner produced ns='xmlns' from one seed's literal '<b xmlns:f="">' bytes, but cannot directly confirm via lineage that grimoire's structural mutators (vs the shared I2S stage) assembled it.

signature.mechanism_summary: Grimoire's GrimoireRecursiveReplacementMutator splices generalized XML/DTD token structure including an empty attribute value, driving an empty string into the entity decoder so str==last and the false branch executes; cmplog's byte-level havoc never produces this structural arrangement.
signature.one_line: Grimoire splices empty-attribute XML to produce empty entity-decode string at str==last

analysis.summary_one_line: An empty (zero-length) entity/string reaching xmlStringDecodeEntitiesInt so str==last on entry and the str<last test is false.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A structurally well-formed DTD document (<?xml ?> + <!DOCTYPE a SYSTEM "dtds/127772.dtd"> + element/attribute body) that drives the parser deep enough to call xmlStringDecodeEntitiesInt with a zero-length string (str==last), so the line-2650 `if (str < last)` test takes its false branch and falls into `c = 0` at line 2653.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: All five winner-resolving seeds are grimoire-discovered and carry intact XML/DTD scaffolding -- `<?xml version="1.0"?>`, `<!DOCTYPEa SYSTEM "dtds/127772.dtd">`, `<a><b ...="">` -- produced by GrimoireRecursiveReplacementMutator splicing generalized token/gap structure; an empty attribute value (`=""`) yields a zero-length decode string so str==last and the false (else) branch executes. The winner hits the function 44 times vs the loser's 9 and exercises the false side (line 2650 W: T=6 F=38) where the loser never does (L: T=9 F=0).
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: The cmplog loser-blocking seeds reach xmlStringDecodeEntitiesInt only 9 times and every entry has a non-empty decode string, so str<last is always true (line 2650 L: T=9 F=0, never F). cmplog's byte-level havoc + I2S substitution mutates DTD/path literals (e.g. the `127772.xml`/`.dsd` filename bytes) but does not assemble the structural arrangement that produces an empty entity body reaching this function.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: grimoire's GeneralizationStage + Grimoire structural mutators (GrimoireRecursiveReplacementMutator etc.) recombine generalized XML/DTD token structure to construct documents that drive an empty string into the entity decoder; cmplog shares I2S with grimoire, so the single-technique delta is the structural/grammar-free recombination, not comparison-solving.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the false side of `if (str < last)` in xmlStringDecodeEntitiesInt, taken when str==last (empty decode string).
The winner takes the else/false branch (c = 0) which the loser never reaches.

Winner resolved 10/10 while loser blocked 9/10 at this branch.

  loser:  resolved=1/10  blocked=9  unreached=0

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness whose only path into xmlStringDecodeEntitiesInt requires a non-empty decode string (str<last always true), yet grimoire still resolves the line-2650 false branch significantly more than cmplog -- showing the divergence is not driven by structurally reaching an empty entity string.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: The causal link from a grimoire-spliced empty attribute value (="") to a zero-length string entering this specific decoder is inferred from source + seed bytes, not directly traced through the call chain.

signature.mechanism_summary: Grimoire's GeneralizationStage and structural mutators splice/delete/recurse generalized token runs to collapse SYSTEM-id fields to empty strings (leading NUL), a structural degeneration cmplog's byte-level I2S cannot produce.
signature.one_line: Grimoire collapses SYSTEM-id to empty NUL; cmplog stuck on non-empty literals

analysis.summary_one_line: Reach xmlBuildURI with an empty systemId string (*URI==0) so the false branch (ret=0) is taken.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A DOCTYPE SYSTEM declaration whose systemId, after URI-building setup, yields an empty URI string at xmlBuildURI so *URI==0 and the false arm (ret=0) executes; this requires degenerate/generalized SYSTEM-id bytes rather than a normal concrete filename like a non-empty "dtds/1..." path.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Grimoire winner seeds carry generalized/spliced structural mutations (lineage shows GrimoireRandomDeleteMutator,GrimoireExtensionMutator,GrimoireRecursiveReplacementMutator) producing degenerate URI fields (leading 27 ' quote, repeated %%% and ///////// runs) that collapse the systemId to an empty string, so the branch resolves on all 10/10 trials.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: cmplog loser seeds are stuck on a fixed concrete structure beginning 06 00 00 00 31 32 37 37 37 32 2e 78 6d 6c ("127772.xml") with a non-empty "dtds/1..." SYSTEM path, which always yields a non-empty URI so *URI!=0 (true branch) and the false side stays blocked 8/10 trials; cmplog's havoc+I2S mutations cannot restructure this into the empty-systemId shape.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: Both fuzzers share the same input-to-state comparison-solving stage, so it is not the delta; the winner is enabled by grimoire's GeneralizationStage plus structural Grimoire mutators (GrimoireExtension/RecursiveReplacement/RandomDelete) that splice generalized token/gap structures into the degenerate SYSTEM-id shape cmplog never reaches.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: The claim that the winning false arm specifically requires an EMPTY systemId string (rather than some other structural property of grimoire's degenerate inputs) is inferred from source plus byte shape, not from a direct dump of the *URI value at the call.

signature.mechanism_summary: Grimoire structural splice/delete mutators embed NUL bytes in URI/DOCTYPE regions, collapsing the reference to empty and leaving ref==NULL; cmplog cannot manufacture this structural emptiness from its fixed non-empty SYSTEM literals.
signature.one_line: Grimoire embeds NUL in URI producing null ref; cmplog stuck on non-empty SYSTEM

analysis.summary_one_line: Winner needs an empty (first-byte-NUL) systemId URI so *URI is false, ret=0, and ref stays NULL.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A document whose external-entity systemId resolves to an empty URI string: the byte handed to xmlBuildURI as URI begins with a NUL so *URI==0 at line 1921, skipping xmlCreateURI() and leaving ref==NULL. The reference must be empty while a non-empty base is still parsed (so ret==0 at 1947 and control reaches line 1952 with ref==NULL).
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Grimoire winner seeds carry NUL bytes inside the URI/DOCTYPE region (e.g. seed 3 byte 0x0b = 00, seed 5 byte 0x0c = 00) produced by structural splice/delete of generalized token runs; at d=1 line 1921 the winner takes *URI false 30 times (T=0 F=30) and consequently L1952 true (T=11), executing the empty-reference body at lines 1956-1962.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: cmplog loser seeds are well-formed DOCTYPE documents with a non-empty SYSTEM literal ('dtds/1...', '127772.xml'); at d=1 line 1921 the loser takes *URI true (T=30 F=0), so xmlCreateURI() runs, ref is non-NULL, and line 1952 is always false (T=0 F=20) — the true branch is never reached, blocking for 20.80h.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: grimoire's GeneralizationStage replaces concrete byte runs with <GAP> placeholders and its structural mutators (GrimoireRecursiveReplacementMutator/GrimoireRandomDeleteMutator) splice and delete whole token/gap segments, which can collapse the systemId field to empty (leading NUL) while keeping the surrounding DOCTYPE grammar valid. Plain cmplog I2S only substitutes logged CMP operands at byte matches and cannot manufacture this structural emptiness, so it stays stuck on non-empty literals.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocked true branch at uri.c:1952 requires ref==NULL, which only happens when *URI is false (empty URI) at line 1921.
When *URI is empty the winner-only path sets ret=0 without creating ref.
The empty-reference body (the winning true side at 1952) is executed only by the winner.
At line 1921 the winner takes *URI false 30 times while the loser takes it true 30 times.
At the blocker line 1952 the winner takes the true branch 11 times while the loser never does.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L1952  T=11 F=0  T=0 F=20  if (ref == NULL) {  <-- BLOCKER

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: The exact byte mechanism by which grimoire produces an empty (leading-NUL) systemId is inferred from embedded NUL bytes in winner seeds plus the L1921 false-side counts, not from a directly observed empty-string-at-call-site trace.

signature.mechanism_summary: Grimoire's structural mutators diversify DOCTYPE/SYSTEM-URI structure into varied degenerate forms (empty paths, degenerate authorities) that repeatedly drive ref to NULL; cmplog is pinned to one non-empty fixed template and never restructures it.
signature.one_line: Grimoire diversifies URI structures to repeatedly reach null ref; cmplog pinned to template

analysis.summary_one_line: An XML document whose external-entity systemId/URI parses such that ref is NULL or that drives many xmlBuildURI resolutions, reaching the if(ref!=NULL) false side.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A structurally varied XML document whose DOCTYPE/SYSTEM external-entity URIs reach xmlBuildURI along paths where ref ends up NULL (empty/degenerate reference URI) so the cleanup test if(ref!=NULL) takes its false side; the winner exercises this branch heavily (hitcount ~861) by generating many distinct URI/DOCTYPE structures, not just one fixed dtds path.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Grimoire-resolved seeds carry diverse generalised URI+DOCTYPE structures (e.g. 'http://www.w...' followed by '<?l?><!DOCTYPEa SYSTEM "">' and degenerate forms like 'http://#.//0=//') that drive xmlBuildURI into the empty-/null-ref code path; winner resolved 10/10 with branch hitcount 861 and the d=1 L1921 split shows the winner taking the empty-URI false side (F=30).
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Cmplog seeds are pinned to a single near-fixed template '127772.xml ... <?xml version="1.0"?> ... <!DOCTYPE a SYSTEM "dtds/1...' across all blocking seeds, so it keeps re-parsing the same systemId shape and stays on the ref!=NULL true side; loser resolved only 2/10, blocked 8, branch hitcount 1.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: Both fuzzers share the full cmplog/I2S stack, so I2S substitution cannot be the discriminator; the winning technique is grimoire's GeneralizationStage plus the Grimoire structural mutators, which recombine generalised token/gap structures to synthesize the structurally varied DOCTYPE/SYSTEM-URI documents that reach the ref==NULL false side, whereas cmplog's havoc+I2S cannot restructure the fixed dtds template.

evidence_trail.claim: At xmlBuildURI depth 1, the winner takes the empty-URI/else path (F) while the loser takes the *URI true path, the upstream condition that determines whether ref stays NULL.
Winner-resolving grimoire seeds carry structurally varied URI + DOCTYPE content.
Winner seeds include degenerate URI shapes distinct from any fixed dtds path.
Loser cmplog seeds are pinned to a single fixed 127772.xml / version=1.0 / dtds template.
Grimoire's GeneralizationStage replaces concrete byte runs with gap placeholders and its structural mutators recombine those generalised structures.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness where cmplog (with I2S but no generalization) reaches the empty/null-ref xmlBuildURI false side at the same rate as grimoire, showing structural recombination is not required to vary the DOCTYPE/SYSTEM-URI shape.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: The seed bytes show grimoire produced more varied URI/DOCTYPE structures, but I cannot directly prove from the prompt that this structural diversity (rather than throughput or luck) is what drives ref to NULL, since the exact ref==NULL parse condition is inferred from source flow.
